issue_title: Post Logout Redirect URIs "+" behavior is inconsistent with other usages (i.e. Web Origins)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Post Logout URIs can be defined in the Admin Console. For "legacy" Keycloak installations when they are migrated, the default value is "+".

As a consumer using a legacy installation that was migrated, I saw "+" in the Post Logout Redirect URI list and assumed it worked much like the Web Origins, where I could add additional values and the "+" list would be expanded and include my additional values. My natural feeling then was to just add my "post logout" URI and keep the "+".

However, in this case, the "+" is treated specially - it must be the first in the list and it does not expand the list of redirect URIs to include any additional added. To my surprise, "+" means that regardless of any other value in the Post Logout Redirect URI list, it will ONLY return the values in the Redirect URIs for the client. This is unexpected behavior compared to other existing features using the "+" nomenclature.

### Version

latest 23.x branch

### Expected behavior

"+" in Post Logout Redirect URIs is treated similarly to other areas (e.g. Web Origins) where it is expanded and includes any new, distinct entities and that it doesn't matter where in the list of Post Logout Redirect URIs where the "+" is. Additional definitions of "+" should likely be ignored.

For example:

Scenario 1:
```
  Redirect URIs:
    https://foo.example.com
    https://foo1.example.com

  Post logout redirect URIs:
    https://someother.foo.example.com
    +

  Net allowed Post Logout Redirect URIs:
    https://foo.example.com
    https://foo1.example.com
    https://someother.foo.example.com
    
  Logout allowed for all URIs.
```
```
Scenario 2:
  Redirect URIs:
    https://foo.example.com
    https://foo1.example.com

  Post logout redirect URIs:
    +
    https://someother.foo.example.com
    +

  Net allowed Post Logout Redirect URIs:
    https://foo.example.com
    https://foo1.example.com
    https://someother.foo.example.com

  Logout allowed for all URIs.

```

### Actual behavior

Scenario 1:
 Receive Invalid redirect uri page when using the additional post logout redirect URI in an OIDC logout request.
 e.g. `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?post_logout_redirect_uri=https://someother.foo.example.com&client_id=my-client`
 
Scenario 2:
 Receive Invalid redirect uri page when using the additional post logout redirect URI in an OIDC logout request.
  e.g. `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?post_logout_redirect_uri=https://someother.foo.example.com&client_id=my-client`


### How to Reproduce?

1. Start Keycloak
2. Create a standard flow client in Keycloak e.g. `my-client` in a realm (e.g. `master`), you could just use `http://localhost:8080` as the initial redirect URI - actual value doesn't matter.
3. Add `+` as a post logout redirect URI to the client
4. Add `https://www.google.com` as a post logout redirect URI to the client
5. Visit the logout endpoint `http://${HOST_AND_PORT}/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout?post_logout_redirect_uri=https://www.google.com&client_id=my-client`
6. Receive "We are sorry..." page with "Invalid redirect uri"


### Anything else?

There is likely some history as part of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/14884 around the particular decisions in this endpoint, but I don't see a particular reason why "+" could not be expanded and act more similarly to the "Web Origins" behavior.

The "-" behavior would be somewhat ambiguous - I'm not sure if there would be expectation that `-` could be added anywhere in the list to negate any additions, and maybe that should take precedence ?
commit_message:
For post logout redirect URI - Make '+' represent existing redirect URIs and merge with existing post logout redirect URIs

Closes keycloak#25544

Signed-off-by: Joshua Sorah <jsorah@redhat.com>

issue_title: iss not returned as query param in redirect to app when using "prompt=none" and user is not authenticated
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using "prompt=none" in an OIDC auth request, such as

```
http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=js-test&redirect_uri=http://localhost:8080&response_type=code&state=stuff-here&prompt=none
```
And a user is not authenticated, Keycloak redirects back to the application with an "error" response such as

```
http://localhost:8080/?error=login_required&state=stuff-here/
```

However, since KC 23, Keycloak advertises support for RFC 9207, and [per the spec](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9207#section-2.2) it seems like Keycloak should also return the `iss` value as a param as well in this scenario.



### Version

23

### Expected behavior

`iss` value is returned as a query parameter in the response

### Actual behavior

`iss` value is not returned as a query parameter in the response

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start Keycloak (can be on localhost:8080)
2. Create an OIDC client `js-test`
3. Add redirect URI "http://localhost:8080/*"
4. Format an auth url such as `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=js-test&redirect_uri=http://localhost:8080&response_type=code&state=stuff-here&prompt=none` ensuring that `prompt=none` is set.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure 'iss' is returned when 'prompt=none' and user is not authenticated, per RFC9207

Closes keycloak/keycloak#25584

Signed-off-by: Joshua Sorah <jsorah@redhat.com>

issue_title: Failure in KeycloakDistConfiguratorTest's 'missingHostname' check
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

The `missingHostname` test in the `KeycloakDistConfiguratorTest` class is responsible for verifying that the resource does not contain the environment variables from the `OptionCategory.HOSTNAME` category.

These variables are populated via the `OptionMapper` in `KeycloakDistConfigurator`, which prefixes them with "KC_", converts them to uppercase, and uses the `StringUtil` from `smallrye-config` to replace all non-alphanumeric characters with underscores. Therefore, the received variables will be:

- `KC_HOSTNAME`
- `KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN`
- `KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN_URL`
- `KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT`
- `KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT_BACKCHANNEL`

After processing, an environment variable internally cannot have a hyphen in its name, as it will be replaced.

In the test, a String.equals comparison is used [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ae04b954a6cf57a1c2a0ec01fa2d42bba11abb26/operator/src/test/java/org/keycloak/operator/testsuite/unit/KeycloakDistConfiguratorTest.java#L224) with `KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT-BACKCHANNEL` (with hypen), so in this case, it will always be false.

The test will never correctly detect if the resource contains `spec.hostname.strictBackchannel`.


### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

The test must correctly detect if the resource contains `spec.hostname.strictBackchannel`.

### Actual behavior

The test will never correctly detect if the resource contains `spec.hostname.strictBackchannel`.

### How to Reproduce?

Run the `KeycloakDistConfiguratorTest.missingHostname` test with a resource containing `spec.hostname.strictBackchannel`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Fixes KeycloakDistConfiguratorTest missingHostname test case. (#25643)

Closes #25642

issue_title: Removing all group attributes no longer works with keycloak-admin-client (java)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client/java

### Describe the bug

With Keycloak 22.x and previous versions, it was possible to remove all group attributes like this:

```
var group = groupResource.toRepresentation();
group.setAttributes(Collections.emptyMap());
groupResource.update(group);
```

This does no longer work with Keycloak 23.0.3. The group attributes remain unchanged.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

All group attributes should be removed.

### Actual behavior

The group attribues remain unchanged.

### How to Reproduce?

See example code in the description.

### Anything else?

Seems this has been introduced by this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/22700.
Because there is now this annotation on GroupRepresentation
`@JsonInclude(JsonInclude.Include.NON_EMPTY)`

This annotation causes the empty attributes not to be included in the request, and being ignored on the server, because the server removes attributes only when they are empty, not null.
commit_message:
Bugfix for: Removing all group attributes no longer works with keycloak-admin-client (java)

Closes #25677

Signed-off-by: Daniel Fesenmeyer <daniel.fesenmeyer@bosch.com>

issue_title: `/admin/realms/{realm-name}/ui-ext/realms` endpoint leaks realms the user doesn't have access to see
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The `/admin/realms/{realm-name}/ui-ext/realms` endpoint shows realms the user doesn't have access to.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

This endpoint should only show the user realms they have access/permission to see.

### Actual behavior

It appears to permit an admin (i.e. all `realm-management` roles) for a specific realm the permission to see all realms.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup:
1. Run Keycloak 23.0.3 in `start-dev` mode.
2. Navigate to the Admin UI and log into the master realm as the default admin user
3. Create `test1` realm and `test2` realm.
4. Create an `admin` user in each `test1` and `test2` realm, and create a password.
5. Grant those new admin users all `realm-management` roles in their respective realms
![Screenshot from 2023-12-18 18-44-04](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/244253/8d3f38b5-7bb9-4599-9277-3f0f2212e030)

Reproduce
1. Logout of any sessions for Keycloak
2. Go to the admin console for the new realm you created (e.g. `http://localhost:8080/admin/test1/console`)
3. Log in as the new admin user you created.
4. Click the realms dropdown and see all realms
5. Use the browser devtools to see all the realms returned from the endpoint
![Screenshot from 2023-12-18 18-44-56](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/244253/5ab840db-c610-42f3-b6b6-9e6453264a58)
![Screenshot from 2023-12-18 18-45-08](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/244253/30c30741-2476-48ae-ad82-ff5ebfcbac0a)


### Anything else?

I didn't send this to the security email, because it only pertains to this UI endpoint, and there is no actual CRUD activity you can do on entities.

Also tried on nightly with the same result.
commit_message:
added permission checking to ui-ext realm resource so realm names are not leaked to users without the appropriate permissions. #25679 (#25683)

Closes: #25392
Closes: #25679

Signed-off-by: Garth <244253+xgp@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Custom Validator is never executed when UserProfileContext is UPDATE_EMAIL
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

With `DECLARATIVE_USER_PROFILE` and `UPDATE_EMAIL` enabled, I add a `validation` on the `email` attribute as follow:

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/2890843/e4508fe8-e48c-465c-9600-baf971281145)

The Validator is never triggered in an `UPDATE_EMAIL` context.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

The Validator should be triggered, even within the  `UPDATE_EMAIL` context.

### Actual behavior

The Validator is never triggered in an `UPDATE_EMAIL` context.

### How to Reproduce?

- Enable `DECLARATIVE_USER_PROFILE` and `UPDATE_EMAIL`
- Enable `User Profile` on the realm
- Add a validation on the `email` attribute
- See it ignored when changing the email via the `UPDATE_EMAIL` form

### Anything else?

Seems to be caused by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/810ebf4efd452a9f3a31df3847301d1a6deaba72/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java#L269-L273, introduced in a6137b9b86820e5899652d6ddd483f704f7f6b3c .

issue_title: Backchannel logout token is missing the "exp" claim
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using backchannel logout, the sent logout token does not contain the required `exp` claim.

I observed this while using a Quarkus-based application with having backchannel configured. Quarkus complains, when receiving the backchannel request:
```
Back channel logout request for the tenant Default received
Verification of the token issued to client shop has failed: No Expiration Time (exp) claim present.
Back channel logout token is invalid
```
So, Quarkus is properly implemented by following [OIDC Backchannel Logout spec, chapter 2.4](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-backchannel-1_0.html#LogoutToken), but Keycloak doesn't send the `exp` claim.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

The logout token must contain the `exp` claim, as mentioned in the [OIDC Backchannel Logout spec, chapter 2.4](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-backchannel-1_0.html#LogoutToken)

### Actual behavior

The logout token does _NOT_ contain the `exp` claim.

Here's a dump of a current logout token payload:
```
{
  "iat": 1703156525,
  "jti": "a2836698-7660-44e8-a9f1-d27d8514b311",
  "iss": "http://localhost:8080/realms/bookshop",
  "aud": "shop",
  "sub": "a6df2d13-8e94-404b-addc-53e248afc95c",
  "typ": "Logout",
  "sid": "2b6eaf9b-990c-4733-a048-b8e8f28612b3",
  "events": {
    "http://schemas.openid.net/event/backchannel-logout": {}
  }
}
```


### How to Reproduce?

Configure any client with a proper backchannel logout url, login/logout with any user from Keycloak and observe/investigate the sent logout token.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OIDC Protocol Mappers with same claim
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

If you have configured two(or more) _OIDC protocol mappers_ with the same _Token Claim Name_, claim will contain only one mapper value!
Claim value must be unique (not allowed duplication values) combination of both mappers value. From this behaviour we need to exclude primary claims ("name", "given_name", "family_name", "preferred_username", "email").

This works for attribute value in _SAML protocol mapper_.

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

If you have configured two(or more) _OIDC protocol mappers_ with the same _Token Claim Name_, claim value will be combination of both mappers value.

### Actual behavior

If you have configured two(or more) _OIDC protocol mappers_ with the same _Token Claim Name_, claim will contain only one mapper value!

### How to Reproduce?

Create two _OIDC protocol mappers_ with same _Token Claim Name_ and _Add to access token_ equal to true.
Get access token and see claim value.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Incorrect JSON format returned in case of existing user (with user federation)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When Keycloak is configured with an LDAP user federation and a user of this federation already exists as a local account (when email is username and therefore unique), the API request GET /users returns the following payload (version 23.0.3) : 
```json
{
	"error": "unknown_error"
}
```
In version 23.0.0, when I first discovered this error, it returns a wrongfully formed payload such as : 
```json
[
    {
		"id": "id",
		"createdTimestamp":  1703277986,
		"username": "username",
		"enabled": true,
		"totp": false,
		"emailVerified": false,
		"firstName": "firstName",
		"lastName": "lastName",
		"email": "email",
		"federationLink": "federationLink",
		"attributes": {
			"LDAP_ENTRY_DN": [
				"LDAP_ENTRY_DN"
			],
			"createTimestamp": [
				"createTimestamp"
			],
			"modifyTimestamp": [
				"modifyTimestamp"
			],
			"LDAP_ID": [
				"LDAP_ID"
			]
		},
		"disableableCredentialTypes": [],
		"requiredActions": [],
		"notBefore": 0,
		"access": {
			"manageGroupMembership": true,
			"view": true,
			"mapRoles": true,
			"impersonate": true,
			"manage": true
		}
	},
	{
		"id": "id",
		"createdTimestamp": 1703277986,
		"username": "username",
		"enabled": true,
		"totp": false,
		"ema{"error":"unknown_error"}
```

Moreover, in admin UI, the following message appears when listing all users : 
> No users found, could be due to wrongly configured federated provider unknown_error

If we search for the culprit user in UI (that I finally found), Keycloak returns nothing.

### Version

Tested on 23.0.0 and 23.0.3

### Expected behavior

Returning an appropriate list of users (as because of one user, all the list can't be accessible), or a more specific error (in API and UI)

### Actual behavior

In API, GET /users returns only :  
```json
{
	"error": "unknown_error"
}
```

In UI, following error is shown : 
 > No users found, could be due to wrongly configured federated provider unknown_error

### How to Reproduce?

Set username as email in realm, then add a local user with an email that exists in your LDAP database.
Then add the LDAP user federation (READ_ONLY, with option importing users).
Finally try to list all users, or request them via API

### Anything else?

I saw a very similar issue #23396 but was closed as the behavior was similar but the origin not (and it hasn't reproduced in 23.0.3)

issue_title: Space in realm name breaks initial console uris
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Realm names can include spaces, but doing so will create a realm with broken console clients.

The console client uris (including the redirect uris) will contain the unencoded realm name, but they need to include the encoded realm name as the redirect uris will break otherwise.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

console clients should be created with valid client uris.

### Actual behavior

The generated redirect uris need to be fixed manually to include the encoded realm name.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create Realm called "Test Realm"
2. The `account` client has the redirect uri `/realms/Test Realm/account/*`
3. The `account-console` client has the redirect uri `/realms/Test Realm/account/*`
4. The `security-admin-console` client has the redirect uri `/admin/Test Realm/console/*`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Encode realm name in console URIs (#29102)

Before this fix console uris (including the client redirect uris) did not contain the url encoded realm name and therefore were invalid.

closes #25807

issue_title: Loosing refresh token with Google Identity Provider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

This issue relates to user authentification using google identity provider with `Request refresh token` and `Store Token` activated .

As state in the [google documentation](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/token-types#refresh), they deliver a refresh token only once at the first user connexion.
At that time it is well store by keycloak in the federated_identity table. But at the second user login the refresh token is not preserved and replaced with the newly obteined json containing only the access token.
This is annoying because the only way to get the refresh token back is to ask for the user to remove the app from his google account !

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

Refresh token should be preserved accross user connexion

### Actual behavior

Refresh token is lost after second login

### How to Reproduce?

1. Login using IDP google with `Request refresh token` and `Store Token` activated. You can see the refresh token on the federated_identity  table.
2. Logout
3. Login again : the refresh token is lost

### Anything else?

_No response_

Add check to prevent deserialization issues when the context token is not an AccessTokenResponse.

- also adds a test for the refresh token on first login scenario.

issue_title: Infinispan Cache(embedded) data is not being updated during mergeView event
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area infinispan ### Describe the bug We have a Quarkus keycloak(version) statefulset with 2 replicas. We intentionally set the num_discovery_runs=1 to simulate network partitions. By setting num_discovery_runs to such a low value for kubernetes based Jgroups Discovery protocol, we can simulate both the replicas initially considered themself as master. Later due to merge protocol of Jgroups these instances able to view eachother and the view reconciles. We update the master realm header only in one of the replica, before the view reconciled. We are updating the realm header via following rest call `curl -g -X PUT "https://${POD1_IP}:${KC_HTTPS_PORT}${KEYCLOAK_PATH}/admin/realms/master/" -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TKN}" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"displayName":"My Custom Realm displayName", "displayNameHtml": "
My Custom Realm displayName
"}'` We are observing that only one instance of keycloak is getting updated with this change and other instance is not getting updated ever in the lifetime of that keycloak cluster. We check the change in the displayName with below rest call. `curl -vk https://${POD2_IP}:${KC_HTTPS_PORT}${KEYCLOAK_PATH}/admin/realms/master/ -H "Authorization: Bearer $TKN" | jq -r '.displayName'` We think Realm caches are not getting synced after the rebalance of membership. Although we have simulated this for the realm cache but we think the same issue is applicable for any other local or distributed cache if keycloak server does not explicitly handle the network partitions scenario. Refer - http://www.jgroups.org/manual5/index.html#HandlingNetworkPartitions We are using the default Infinispan cache configuration for kubernetes. ### Version 22.0.5 ### Expected behavior Once the views are merged after network partitions. Data in the caches should refresh there content from database where applicable or merge there contents(e.g. Authorization tokens) ### Actual behavior Data in the caches are becoming divergent after network partitions, even though the views were merged at the Infinispan level. ### How to Reproduce? Please refer the description. ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: RealmResourceProvider is not working as expected since version 23.0.0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

After upgrading to version 23.0.0, I noticed that a custom RealmResourceProvider that I am implementing and deploying as  an extension is misbehaving:
- instead of returning the HTTP 200 OK response I am expecting (which is also the case if I downgrade to some version prior to 23.0.0), it is returning the following:
```
Status: HTTP 405
Response body:  {"error":"HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed"}
```
- I double checked that the method I am using from curl and also from the app matches the annotation on my custom resource (the resource returned from the `getResource()` method in `RealmResourceProvider`), and the method is the same, so I figured this could be due to some Quarkus issue. 
After I activated the debug logging, it turns out the issue is happening at the level of the `quarkus-resteasy-reactive` library. Here is the stack trace:
```
2024-01-03 19:04:09,555 DEBUG [org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext] (executor-thread-1) Restarting handler chain for exception exception: jakarta.ws.rs.WebApplicationException: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.ResourceLocatorHandler.handle(ResourceLocatorHandler.java:71)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:150)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:145)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:576)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)

2024-01-03 19:04:09,555 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) Error response 405: jakarta.ws.rs.WebApplicationException: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.ResourceLocatorHandler.handle(ResourceLocatorHandler.java:71)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:150)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:145)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:576)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

The error is happening here  https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/independent-projects/resteasy-reactive/server/runtime/src/main/java/org/jboss/resteasy/reactive/server/handlers/ResourceLocatorHandler.java#L70. I also did some debugging and it seems like for some reason the resource class is not found by the method findTarget here https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/independent-projects/resteasy-reactive/server/runtime/src/main/java/org/jboss/resteasy/reactive/server/handlers/ResourceLocatorHandler.java#L97 

I was not able to reproduce the error on a plain standalone quarkus (version 3.2.9.Final which is the one used here by Keycloak) project:

```
@Path("/hello")
public class GreetingResource {

    @Path("{lang}")
    public Object hello(@PathParam("lang") String lang) {
        if ("fr".equals(lang)) {
            return new BonjourResource();
        } 
        
        return new HalloResource();
    }

    static class HalloResource{

        @Produces(MediaType.TEXT_PLAIN)
        @GET
        public String hallo() {return "Hallo from RestEasy";}
    }

    static class BonjourResource{

        @GET
        @Produces(MediaType.TEXT_PLAIN)
        public String hallo() {return "Bonjour from RestEasy";}
    }
}
``` 

Everything works as expected with the above, so this could be due to some misconfiguration in the current Keycloak version. 

The only solution I found was to annotate my resource class with `@Provider`.
 

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

Let's assume, I am using the official Keycloak example https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/tree/main/examples/providers/rest. 

After deploying it, I would expect  a GET HTTP call to `/realms/test/hello` to return 200 with `"Hello test"` (given that the realm name is test)

### Actual behavior

I am getting a HTTP 405 status with the following response:

```
{"error":"HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed"}
```

unless https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/examples/providers/rest/src/main/java/org/keycloak/examples/rest/HelloResourceProvider.java is annotated with `@Provider`

### How to Reproduce?

To reproduce the issue, the keyloak official rest provider example needs to packaged and deployed in the `providers/` folder of a local running Keycloak distribution. After restarting it, a realm needs to be created and then   `curl http://localhost:8080/realms/test/hello` should return the response mentionned above. 


The issue can also be reproduced on my api key demo project https://github.com/zak905/keycloak-api-key-demo by:
- cloning the project
- removing or commenting the `@Provider` annotation from the resource class https://github.com/zak905/keycloak-api-key-demo/blob/master/api-key-module/src/main/java/com/gwidgets/resources/ApiKeyResource.java#L11
- running the following in a shell: `mvn -f api-key-module package && docker-compose up auth-server`
- finally running from a different terminal `curl -v "localhost:8080/auth/realms/example/check"`



### Anything else?

_No response_

Create an empty group with via ldapmodify or something and see how this create an empty member: entry which is not removed once a member is added and cause the LDAP provide to crash during sync with the above error.

issue_title: Optimize handling of terms and conditions during registration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

After having updated to Keycloak 22 we tried the [newly introduced feature](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/25506) for including terms and conditions directly into the registration page. Unfortunately this feature does not integrate well with how required actions work in general and on other places (for details see below).

### Version

22.0.5

### Expected behavior

After a new user accepted TaC on the registration page, the system should:

- Properly persist the user attribute with the timestamp.
- Not display the subsequent second page showing TaC (again).

### Actual behavior

During testing, we detected two possible deficits:

- It seems that the user attribute with the timestamp is not set automatically. See also [this discussion](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/23653) which might be related to that.
- The separate page for terms and conditions is still shown after the user submitted the first page. We even tried adding a hidden form field carrying the attribute, but this workaround did not work out.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Setup registration flow like [described in the manual](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#proc-requiring-tac-agreement-at-registration_server_administration_guide)
2. Also configure TaC as a required action.
3. Click through registration process.

### Anything else?

For the moment, we decided to disable the direct inclusion of TaC again in favour of the separate page.

Integrate registration with terms and conditions required action
Closes #25891

issue_title: Keycloak HA Guide uses token for cross-site setup that expires
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The [command in the docs](https://www.keycloak.org/high-availability/deploy-infinispan-kubernetes-crossdc) creates a short-lived token which will expire within one hour by default: 

```
kubectl create token -n keycloak xsite-sa
``` 

Once the token expires, the Operator won't be able to set up the xsite connection any more. 

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The docs should use a token that doesn't expire

### Actual behavior

The token expires.

### How to Reproduce?

Retrieve the token and put it into the site `jwt.io` to analyze it.

### Anything else?

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-benchmark/issues/663
commit_message:
High Availability Docs: use unbounded token for cross-site connection

Expirable tokens are more secure but it requires manual intervention to
create and share them when they expire.

I have updated the documentation to use non-expirable tokens.

Closes #25909

```
INFO  [org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry] (executor-thread-44) Creating new LDAP Store for the LDAP storage provider: 'ldap', LDAP Configuration: {fullSyncPeriod=[-1], pagination=[false], startTls=[false], connectionPooling=[true], usersDn=[ou=people,dc=test,dc=keycloak,dc=local], cachePolicy=[DEFAULT], useKerberosForPasswordAuthentication=[false], importEnabled=[true], enabled=[true], usernameLDAPAttribute=[uid], changedSyncPeriod=[-1], bindDn=[uid=keycloak,dc=test,dc=keycloak,dc=local], vendor=[rhds], uuidLDAPAttribute=[uid], connectionUrl=[ldap://quarkus-kc-cluster-1-ldap], allowKerberosAuthentication=[false], syncRegistrations=[true], authType=[simple], useTruststoreSpi=[ldapsOnly], usePasswordModifyExtendedOp=[false], trustEmail=[true], userObjectClasses=[inetOrgPerson, organizationalPerson], rdnLDAPAttribute=[uid], editMode=[READ_ONLY], validatePasswordPolicy=[false]}, binaryAttributes: []
```

As far as I can tell, in previous versions (up to 21), this message was logged only once.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

The message is logged only once, when the configuration is set up the first time.

### Actual behavior

The message is generated on every user login, greatly polluting the log output.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a LDAP federation and monitor the log after every user login.

### Anything else?

If other configuration details (e.g., Infinispan configurations) are relevant, I could link them (they are public), but from what I can tell from [the code](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/904e688400cbb4470b15d88ef9f65f03ee1e7c71/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPIdentityStoreRegistry.java#L38), the cache used for lookup is local to Keycloak.
commit_message:
Add migration for the useTruststoreSpi config property in LDAP user storage provider
- legacy `ldapsOnly` value now migrated to `always`.

Closes #25912

issue_title: UI crash after using breadcrumb group navigation during an active group search
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

After searching for a nested group in the groups section, the usage of the breadcrumb navigation (above the group title) ends in a UI crash.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

I'd expect the same behaviour during the `search mode` as it would behave, if I manually navigate to the nested group only using the tree navigation.

### Actual behavior

As soon I use the breadcrumb navigation (after I searched, and selected a child group), the UI crashes and needs to be hard-refreshed.

Before the UI crashes, I see a 404 HTTP error from the API, which is not there, if I manually select the groups from the three view.

I have two groups:

* top-level group (`a997ad23-605e-4b77-927e-c3a0daeb4c11`)
* second-level group (`5a12534d-48fe-4042-804b-9a12e1cde4f9`)

If i select the top-level group from the breadcrumb, the following api call ends in a 404.

```http
GET /admin/realms/Github-Issue/groups/5a12534d-48fe-/children
```
instead of:
```http
GET /admin/realms/Github-Issue/groups/a997ad23-605e-4b77-927e-c3a0daeb4c11/children
```

> It seems, the generated links for the breadcrumb are broken (in search mode). The group id in the path parameter looks like the first part of the second-level group, instead of the group id of the parent. 

As result of the broken links, the UI has problems to handle this.

```javascript
TypeError: b is null
    Fe KeycloakDataTable.tsx:240
    is KeycloakDataTable.tsx:341
    uc useFetch.ts:33
    promise callback*uc/< useFetch.ts:31
    React 10
    a SubGroupsContext.tsx:24
    onClick GroupBreadCrumbs.tsx:38
    O index.js:746
    React 16
    <anonymous> main.tsx:20
react-dom.production.min.js:10:71285
```

![ui-crash](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1324909/270f6b08-1cc5-4d6f-bcd3-a2f5614c4c04)

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a top-level group in the Groups section
2. Create second-level group as child of the top-level group
3. Search for the second-level group via the search bar
4. Select the second-level group from the tree view
5. Try to navigate now via the breadcrumb navigation to the top-level group

### Anything else?

_No response_

fixes navigation when searching for groups (#26053)

issue_title: Declartive user profile. When multiple attributes with options validator are defined and 1 is selected on UI shown that 2 of them have values.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have defined 2 attributes, which have options validator. When i try to set value for one of those attribute on user profile page it selects for both of them.
![a1](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/87321239/a9bcafb9-9f7a-4a78-b46c-17514f9e1fc4)
![a2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/87321239/90a2293d-f7c5-4745-9439-c588ab4e8221)
![a3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/87321239/9c76540a-9e18-40f7-a0d6-2f2400491b59)
![a4](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/87321239/52ea50be-40c0-4687-bff1-822051be38a6)

Small hint: when i click on value which i actually select it shows blue checkmark, on the other dropdown checkmark is not shown. 
![a5](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/87321239/bd8a0cbc-2c42-498a-82b3-f1a07921009e)

Also another way to verify that only 1 value is selected is to define Token Mapper -> UserAttribute. Create mapper for attribute a1 and a2. And if you evalue token you will see value from a1 attribute.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

Whem multiple attributes with validator options are defined and you set **one** of this values to the user, then UI should render as a **selected** only that value you are actually select.

### Actual behavior

Multiple values are shown as a selected, even though you select only one.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Install Keycloak version 23.0.3. 
2. Enable features declarative-user-profile,account3. 
3. Create multiple attributes, minimum 2. 
4. Define validator for those attributes with type **options**
5. Add multiple values 
6. Go to the user and set value to one of the defined attributes
7. Hit save
8. Multiple values in select are shown. 

### Anything else?
 #23905
